questions.question: Why does production image work revolve around inpainting rather than text-to-image?
options: Text-to-image is legally restricted
~70% of billable image work is EDITING — swap a background, remove a logo, regenerate a hand — and from-scratch generation can't keep the rest of the image pixel-identical
Inpainting models are smaller
Clients prefer the word "inpainting"
questions.explanation: The task family: inpainting regenerates inside a mask, outpainting regenerates outside/beyond the canvas, editing regenerates everything while keeping semantic fidelity (SDEdit, InstructPix2Pix).

questions.question: Why does the naive mask-and-replace approach to inpainting fail?
options: Masks can't be applied to latents
Replacing the unmasked region with the forward-diffused clean image at each step leaves the model blind to what's inside the masked region's surroundings — boundary artifacts bleed through
It requires retraining the VAE
It works fine — there's no problem
questions.explanation: The model needs the clean context as an explicit conditioning input, not just a post-hoc pixel paste — that's what the dedicated inpainting architecture provides.

questions.question: How does a proper inpainting model differ architecturally?
options: It uses a second discriminator

The U-Net takes 9 input channels instead of 4: noisy latent (4) + VAE-encoded source image (4) + mask (1), trained on randomly masked images to denoise only the masked region with the clean surroundings as conditioning
It runs two U-Nets in parallel
It operates in pixel space only
questions.explanation: SD-Inpaint, SDXL-Inpaint, and Flux-Fill all use this 9-channel (or analogous) input — the model can "see" the context and produce coherent completions.

questions.question: How does SDEdit edit images with no retraining at all?
options: It edits prompts, not images

Noise the source image up to an intermediate t, then run the reverse chain with a new prompt — t/T ≈ 0.3 gives near-identical results with stylistic tweaks, 0.6 moderate edits preserving coarse structure, 0.9 near-total regeneration
It interpolates two latents linearly
It uses a fine-tuned editing model
questions.explanation: The starting-noise level is a fidelity-vs-freedom dial. InstructPix2Pix is the trained alternative: fine-tuned on (input, instruction, output) triples with separate image and text CFG scales.

questions.question: When would you reach for RePaint?
options: When you need maximum speed

When you only have a standard unconditional diffusion model: at each reverse step it occasionally jumps back to a noisier state and regenerates, avoiding boundary artifacts without any inpainting-specific training
When the mask covers the whole image
When working with GANs instead
questions.explanation: The resampling trick buys coherence with extra compute instead of extra training — the fallback when no 9-channel inpainting checkpoint exists for your base model.
